# Introduction

Localization systems estimate position from physical measurements captured by one or more reference nodes.

In this book, we introduce the main measurement techniques used in practice: Time of Arrival (ToA), Time Difference of Arrival (TDoA), Angle of Arrival/Departure (AoA/AoD), and Received Signal Strength (RSS). Each technique offers a different trade-off between required infrastructure, visibility conditions (LOS/NLOS), and achievable accuracy.

We will compare these techniques and study how they are integrated into complete systems, from the localization receiver that acquires measurements to the positioning engine that computes the user state.
